vocabulary_note: Grades drawn from the 23-grade US-only vocabulary defined in oil_network.commodities. Foreign feedstocks (WCS, Maya, Merey, Bonny Light, Russian ESPO, etc.) are referenced in notes but not represented as grade rows; the generic 'crude' root is used where a slate is materially driven by imports not covered by the vocabulary.

grades.notes: HF Sinclair Puget Sound (ex-Shell Anacortes) is a major rail-served destination for Bakken; light-sweet anchor of the slate.
Puget Sound refineries take ANS via tanker from Valdez; standard PNW feedstock.
Occasional Permian light-sweet via Gulf-to-PNW tanker movements.
WCS heavy from Trans Mountain pipeline at Anacortes is a meaningful share of the slate; not in the US-only vocabulary.
Cushing Connect pipeline (160 kbd JV) directly feeds the Tulsa complex; WTI Cushing is the anchor grade.
Local Oklahoma sweet gathered from regional production.
Mid-continent pipeline access to Bakken via Cushing.
Permian barrels reachable via Cushing.
WCS heavy via Keystone/Cushing routes; not in US-only vocabulary.
Sister plant to Tulsa East on the same Cushing Connect infrastructure.
Local Oklahoma sweet anchored by HF Sinclair's mid-continent gathering.
Permian barrels via Cushing.
Bakken via Cushing storage hub.
Topping plant on the North Slope; processes only Alaska North Slope crude for local fuel needs (diesel, jet, gasoline) for oilfield operations.
Hunt Crude Oil Supply trucks/pipelines blended Mississippi-Alabama sweet to the plant; closest US-vocabulary analog is LLS.
Term/spot barge crude from US Gulf Coast via the Mid-Continent (St. Louis) and Gulf sourcing channels.
Canadian heavy from Alberta/Saskatchewan acquired via rail/pipeline; not in US-only vocabulary.
Marathon disclosed ~58% of crude from North Dakota Bakken; the anchor grade.
Canadian sweet and heavy sour (WCS-equivalent) make up the balance; not in US-only vocabulary.
Kern Energy (ex-Kern Oil) sits in the San Joaquin Valley; Kern County heavy is the natural anchor grade.
Midway-Sunset field is the largest in California and a routine San Joaquin feedstock.
Generic San Joaquin heavy blend.
100 kbd splitter (2x50) supplied by KMCC Eagle Ford gathering; offtake to BP. Dedicated condensate splitter.
Light Eagle Ford streams that fit the splitter envelope.
Bakken reaches Pine Bend via Enbridge mainline and rail.
Pine Bend was purpose-built for WCS / Alberta heavy and remains the largest Canadian-sour processor in the upper Midwest; not in US-only vocabulary.
Phillips 66 Lines WA/80 deliver 120-130 kbd of Permian crude into Borger.
Permian condensate moves on the same gathering systems.
Borger historically pulled barrels from Cushing; legacy Line O retired in favour of Canyon Crossing.
Canyon Crossing pipeline supplies ~60 kbd of heavy crude (API down to 18.5), enabling WCS-equivalent runs at Borger; not in US-only vocabulary.
Corpus Christi plants are the natural home for Eagle Ford light; Flint Hills runs the full Eagle Ford slate.
Condensate stabilisation and splitting are standard at the Corpus complex.
Permian crude reaches Corpus via EPIC and Cactus pipelines.
Permian condensate via Corpus terminal infrastructure.
Twin to the East plant; full Eagle Ford slate.
Eagle Ford condensate via Karnes-to-Corpus pipelines.
Permian crude via EPIC/Cactus.
Permian condensate via Corpus terminal infrastructure.
Mid-continent WTI via Patoka/Wood River pipeline interconnects.
Bakken via Enbridge mainline to Patoka.
253 kbd Illinois refinery processes large volumes of Canadian heavy (WCS-equivalent) per Marathon disclosures; not in US-only vocabulary.
Bakken delivered by rail and Enbridge Line 5/6 system.
Mid-continent WTI via Wood River/Patoka interconnects.
Post-DHOUP (2012, $2.2B Fluor build) the plant processes an additional 80 kbd of heavy sour Canadian crude (WCS-equivalent); not in US-only vocabulary.
Marathon's fact sheet identifies sweet and sour crude including local Utica shale; mid-continent WTI is the dominant sweet barrel.
Bakken via Patoka and rail.
Local Utica shale crude (not separately catalogued in the 23-grade vocabulary) plus modest Canadian sour.
Houston Ship Channel location; direct pipeline access to Houston-priced WTI.
Mars Blend via SPM/onshore connection — standard medium-sour barrel for Texas City.
Poseidon medium-sour from offshore Gulf via pipeline.
BP-operated Thunder Horse production via offshore pipeline to Texas Gulf.
SGC medium-sour as part of Gulf offshore slate.
Eagle Ford light via Texas Gulf gathering.
Permian crude via Longhorn/Cactus.
Norco sits on LOOP/Capline; Louisiana Light Sweet is the anchor sweet barrel.
Mars Blend via LOOP — standard Gulf Coast medium-sour for Valero.
Poseidon medium-sour via offshore Gulf pipeline.
BP Thunder Horse via offshore Gulf pipeline.
SGC medium-sour from Gulf.
WTI via Cactus II/Bayou Bridge into St. James/LOCAP.
Ex-Shell Mobile/Saraland refinery; Alabama-Mississippi sweet is the historic anchor and LLS is the natural US-vocabulary analog.
Gulf Coast access via Mobile-area marine terminals.
Light-sweet-distillate focused plant (high VGO/distillate output); Eagle Ford lights fit the slate envelope.
Asphalt-focused refinery (World Oil / Lunday-Thagard) in South Gate; the slate is California heavy for roofing/paving asphalt.
Trucked Kern County heavy is the standard SoCal asphalt feedstock.
Midway-Sunset San Joaquin heavy as an alternative asphalt feed.
10 kbd asphalt plant runs local Mississippi crude (closest US-vocabulary analog is LLS) plus international cargoes via Vicksburg/Gulf.
International crudes (heavy sour for asphalt) sourced via the Gulf; not in US-only vocabulary.
Marathon disclosure: ~75% of throughput is Alaska North Slope; balance is Cook Inlet sweet.
Marathon disclosure references domestic crude from North Dakota — Bakken via tanker to Nikiski.
Mandan is the closest refinery to the Bakken play and runs almost exclusively Bakken light sweet.
Marathon (ex-Tesoro/Andeavor) Anacortes is a long-standing ANS processor via Valdez tankers.
Anacortes has a rail terminal that brought Bakken into the slate as a major component.
Occasional Permian light-sweet via tanker.
WCS from Trans Mountain pipeline (Burnaby BC) reaches Anacortes; not in US-only vocabulary.
Marathon El Paso fact sheet: ~133 kbd, dedicated Permian pipelines, light-sweet anchored.
Permian condensate moves on the same gathering.
West Texas Light reachable from Permian Basin pipelines.
Mid-continent WTI reachable via Centurion/Basin reversals.
5 kbd naphthenic base-oil unit; runs local Arkansas/N. Louisiana naphthenic-rich sweet crude. LLS is the closest US-vocabulary analog.
50 kbd condensate splitter at Magellan's Corpus terminal, fed by Double Eagle (50/50 with KMI) — Eagle Ford condensate is the dedicated feedstock.
Par discloses all domestic supply comes from Alaska; ANS via tanker is a recurring but not dominant share.
Most barrels are imports (Russia, Indonesia, Saudi Arabia ~70% of total imports historically; Russian cargoes suspended in 2022). The dominant feedstock is not in the US-only vocabulary.
Par Pacific disclosure: refinery purchases crude primarily from Wyoming suppliers; Powder River sweet/Wyoming sweet is the natural Rocky Mountain anchor.
Niobrara Sweet from the DJ/Powder River basin reachable via regional pipelines.
Wyoming asphaltic crude for the asphalt portion of the slate.
Bakken via regional pipelines (e.g. Belle Fourche).
WCS via Express pipeline from Hardisty/Edmonton; not in US-only vocabulary.
Par Pacific disclosure: Bakken + Cold Lake represent >95% of slate. Bakken is the US-vocabulary half.
Marine access at Tacoma allows opportunistic ANS via tanker.
Cold Lake heavy (Canadian) is the second pillar of the Tacoma slate per Par disclosure; not in US-only vocabulary.
Par Pacific disclosure: Powder River Basin sweet, gathered via the Thunder Creek 140-mile system.
Niobrara Sweet adjacent in the DJ/Powder River geology.
PBF disclosure: medium-to-heavy sour crude slate; Mars Blend via LOOP/St James is the medium-sour anchor.
Poseidon medium-sour from Gulf offshore via pipeline.
LLS available at St James as an opportunity barrel.
Thunder Horse medium-sour.
SGC via Gulf offshore pipeline.
Plant was a PDVSA JV until 2015 and is configured for heavy sour (Merey/Maya-class imports via Gulf); not in US-only vocabulary.

PBF Logistics CPI Operations — the refining units have been idled since June 2017; current operation is a terminal/asphalt facility. Any restart slate would be East-Coast imports (Bonny Light, North Sea grades) not in the US-only vocabulary.
PBF Martinez (ex-Shell/Equilon) is a Bay Area full-conversion plant; ANS via long-haul tanker is the anchor sour.
San Joaquin Valley heavy via SJV-to-Bay pipelines.
Kern County heavy via pipeline north.
Midway-Sunset feedstock for the heavy end of the slate.
Permian light-sweet via tanker (water-borne).
Latin American imports (Ecuador Napo / Colombia Vasconia) historically; not in US-only vocabulary.
PBF Paulsboro has rail access; Bakken-by-rail is the cheapest domestic light barrel.
Eagle Ford via tanker from Corpus/Houston.
Mars via Gulf-to-East-Coast tanker (medium-sour).
East Coast medium-sour complex (160 kbd); historically anchored on Bonny Light / Nigerian sweet and Saudi Arabian Light. Dominant feedstock not in US-only vocabulary.
California refinery; pipeline access to Kern County heavy is the standard SoCal feedstock.
Midway-Sunset San Joaquin heavy.
Generic California heavy blend via SJV pipelines.
ANS via Long Beach tanker terminal.
Citgo (PDVSA) Corpus designed for heavy/sour; Mars Blend via Gulf onshore pipelines is the medium-sour anchor.
Poseidon medium-sour via offshore Gulf pipeline.
Citgo Corpus has modified units to process lighter Eagle Ford as a balance barrel.
Thunder Horse medium-sour.
SGC medium-sour from Gulf.
PDVSA-affiliated plant; historically and structurally configured for Venezuelan Merey heavy sour; not in US-only vocabulary.
CITGO Lemont takes Bakken via Enbridge mainline as the light-sweet portion of the slate.
Mid-continent WTI via Patoka pipeline connections.

Citgo disclosure: ~90 kbd of heavy sour Canadian (WCS) crude; sophisticated facility designed for 25-30 API, 2-3% sulfur. Dominant feedstock not in US-only vocabulary; historically also processes Venezuelan Merey when available.
Deer Park has long pulled Mars Blend via Houston-area pipelines as the medium-sour Gulf anchor.
Poseidon medium-sour available via offshore Gulf pipeline.
WTI Houston as the light-sweet balance barrel post-2022.
Thunder Horse medium-sour.

Pemex-owned since 2022; the JV historically committed up to 200 kbd of Mexican Maya heavy sour to the plant. Maya remains the dominant feedstock; not in US-only vocabulary. Plant also blends Canadian and US lights for optimisation.
